detail.description: Provides a message associated with the operation, suitable for displaying to the end user.

operation.description:
Specifies the executed operation. It can be either `delete`, `cancel`, or `expire`.

* `refund` - Refund
* `void` - Void
* `capture` - Capture
* `delete` - Delete
* `cancel` - Cancel
* `expire` - Expire

result.description:
Indicates the outcome of the operation. `success` denotes a successful operation.

* `success` - Success
* `failed` - Failed

amount.description: The specific amount for which the operation was performed.

session_id.description: The session ID of the parent transaction will be included in the webhook payload. This session ID is crucial for associating the webhook event with the original transaction, allowing for accurate tracking and processing.

order_no.description: The 'order_no' field indicates the unique order number of the parent transaction. This identifier is crucial for tracking and managing the related order within its entire lifecycle.

username.description: Required. 150 characters or fewer. Letters, digits and @/./+/-/_ only.

initiator.description: If the operation was performed using Basic Auth (and not an API Key), this field contains the details of the `initiator` who started the operation.

is_sandbox.title: Is Sandbox?
is_sandbox.description: Indicates whether the operation was performed in a test environment or not.

operation.description: Identifies the operation that was executed.

pg_code.description: Represents the `pg_code` of the Payment Gateway settings which was used to perform the operation.

pg_response.description: Contains the raw response from the payment gateway (PG) for the operation (`pg_response`). It will always be a valid JSON.

reference_number.description: A unique reference_number assigned by Ottu for the performed operation. It's also sent to the PG and can be used as a reconciliation parameter.

source.description: Can have one of two values - `input` or `pg`. If `input`, it means the operation was performed in an API call triggered by the merchant. If `pg`, it means the operation was done on the PG management dashboard, and the PG notified Ottu via webhook. The `pg` value will always be notified to the webhook, never in an API call.

success.description: Indicates whether the operation was successful or not (`success=True` or `success=False`).

result.description: The result of the operation - whether it was successful or not.

signature.description: Signature Field: A cryptographic hash used to guarantee data integrity and authenticity during client-server exchanges. This hash ensures that the API payload has not been tampered with, and can only be verified by authorized parties.

timestamp_utc.description: Specifies the time when the operation was performed, in the UTC timezone.

currency_code.description: The specified currency represents the denomination of the transaction.Nevertheless, it doesn't necessarily mandate payment in this exact currency.Due to potential currency conversions or exchanges, the final charge may be in a different currency.

order_no.description: The unique identifier assigned to this payment transaction. It is used for tracking purposes and is set by the merchant or the system.

session_id.description: A unique identifier for each payment transaction, used to maintain the session state during the payment process.

state.description:
The current state of the payment transaction, it helps to understand the progress of the payment.

* `paid` - paid
* `refunded` - refunded
* `refund_queued` - refund_queued
* `refund_rejected` - refund_rejected
* `voided` - voided

txn.description: Every operation results in the creation of a payment transaction (`txn`), which is a child of the payment transaction against which the operation is performed. This child transaction holds all the details of the operation.

allOf.description: Serializer for PaymentTransaction with dynamically generated fields.
allOf.title: PGParams

pg_params.description:
The `pg_params` field contains the details received 
from the payment gateway callback these details are 
provided to us by the gateway after a user has completed 
a payment transaction additionally, `pg_params` 
can include information obtained from an inquiry 
request made to the payment gateway status check API.

extra.description: The extra information for the payment details, which the merchant has sent it in key value form.

detail.description: Provides a message associated with the operation, suitable for displaying to the end user.

operation.description:
* `delete` - delete
* `cancel` - cancel
* `expire` - expire
* `refund` - refund
* `capture` - capture
* `void` - void
* `operation_tracking` - operation_tracking
